title: self_evolve verify: require concrete artifact, not just task completion
description: Change _verify_self_evolve in agents/super/core.py to check that self-evolution produced a concrete artifact (new or modified skill file, modified config, or logged diff) — not just that the task ran and set a state key.
rationale: The reading note's core claim: '别假装做了就有效' — don't pretend that doing something makes it effective. Applied to Mira: _verify_self_evolve almost certainly checks a state key (task ran), which is the same failure mode Cowen names. 'Self-evolution completed' is not the same as 'something actually changed.' The CoT faithfulness thread compounds this — Mira's self-critique outputs are narratively coherent but may be post-hoc rationalization, not tracked behavior change. The weakness log in MEMORY.md confirms the pattern: 'error_acknowledgment: 0.5 — Still not habitual' appears week after week. The reflection runs, the state key is set, nothing changes. The verification is theater.
diff_description: In _verify_self_evolve, after the existing state-key check, add: scan the soul/ directory (agents/shared/soul/) for any file with mtime within the last 2 hours. If no file was touched, return False (or return a warning state). This makes the verifier falsifiable — 'self-evolve ran' only passes if at least one soul file (skill, journal entry, memory update, config) was written. No artifact = no credit. The check is cheap (one os.scandir call) and low-risk since it only tightens an existing verify function without changing task dispatch or agent logic.
source_note: 2026-04-04_cowen-的诚实比大多数增强叙事更有信息量.md
